methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Zeta Surgical is a Series A biotech & health company founded by Raahil Sha and William Gormley, currently valued at $100M post-money on $23M total raised. The valuation analysis triangulates against a comp universe of 3 public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the same sector, including Series B-stage companies like Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), and New Limit ($810M).

Two methods proved applicable. Stage-progression modeling applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement to the $100M base valuation, yielding $200M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies (Eikon, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, Profluent) produces a $810M result, with the cohort range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring was not applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $170M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $505M. The wide dispersion reflects the substantial heterogeneity in Series B peer outcomes and the limited applicability of public multiples without revenue visibility. Key uncertainties include the target's commercial progress relative to the Series B cohort and whether stage-progression or peer-median better captures current market conditions for frontier biotech at this funding stage.
